message:
fix(raptorq): use largest-remainder method for normalize_permille to avoid rounding drift

The previous normalize_permille() assigned N-1 values by truncated
division then gave the entire remainder to the last slot. This could
produce posterior distributions where the last element carried up to
3 extra permille points while earlier elements lost fractional value,
creating visible rounding bias in 4-way decisions.

Replace with normalize_permille_generic<const N>() using the
largest-remainder method: compute all base values by truncated
division, then distribute remaining units one-at-a-time to the
slots with the largest fractional remainders (tie-broken by index).

Also extract normalize_contributor_permille() for 3-element
evidence-contributor distributions with a [0,0,0] zero-total default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
